page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.24xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor architecture running at 2.8 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core operations and memory throughput. The multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests rank in the top 10%, supported by a high-capacity memory subsystem that achieves uncached read bandwidths of 481.08 GB/sec and write bandwidths of 217.77 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks also perform in the top 10%, reaching 197.57 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU performance and image processing fall into the average tier, and text generation for small LLM models is relatively weak at 66.56 tokens per second, though text generation for medium and large models remains in the top tier.

The ARM-based architecture and high memory density offer qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large memory footprints and high-speed local storage without the overhead of x86 licensing. The combination of 768.0 GB of RAM and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD makes this instance suitable for memory-intensive databases, large-scale Redis deployments, multi-threaded data decompression, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.24xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 40 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, with uncached read speeds reaching 481.08 GB/sec. While single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are average to weak, the system excels in multi-threaded tasks, database operations, and Redis caching, where it achieves 197.57 million operations per second. This hardware profile provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads, making it highly suitable for large databases, caching layers, software compilation, and medium-to-large scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.24xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 768 GB RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six dedicated cores, massive local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core database performance.

768.0 GB of system memory paired with 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing
